extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo demolished the 8.6km Turin ITT by 10 seconds over teammate AFFINI Edoardo, setting early GC gaps before sprint stages.
tactical_patterns: AFFINI Edoardo's first-year Jumbo-Visma performance suggests team switching unlocked ITT capacity not shown at Bike Exchange previously.
EVENEPOEL Remco placing sixth despite grand tour debut inexperience signals his prologue specialists credentials translate immediately to three-week racing.
ALMEIDA João finishing 7.3 seconds down indicates deliberate pacing strategy to avoid overcommitment before mountain stages unlock his climbing advantage.
FOSS Tobias taking hot seat before AFFINI displaced him reveals Jumbo-Visma depth allows tactical flexibility in GC protection roles.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: 10-second margin on 8.6km course demonstrates flat power output remains unmatched even against specialist ITT field
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: positive: second place in first Jumbo-Visma year after Bike Exchange stint suggests environmental training upgrade unlocked hidden watts
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: sixth place in grand tour debut without specific preparation shows natural chrono ability transfers under fatigue pressure
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: 7.3-second deficit suggests controlled effort rather than maximum output to preserve climbing legs for mountain stages
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: high GC placement after 2020 abandonment indicates complete recovery and renewed ambition for classification battle
rider_signals.FOSS Tobias: positive: held hot seat against multiple specialists before AFFINI displaced him showing Tour de l'Avenir form carries to senior racing
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: negative: disappointing performance one week after Swiss ITT win suggests peak timing miscalculation or travel fatigue impact
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: satisfactory ride indicates conservative power management prioritizing three-week durability over stage-one time gains
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: similar time to BERNAL suggests both climbing specialists adopted identical conservative prologue pacing strategy
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: performed admirably for veteran status indicating maintained TT discipline despite age-related power decline elsewhere
